title: Resume Import — Upload to Career Entries

list.name: importId
list.label: Import ID
list.description: Filter per-import drill-down panels to a single importId (UUID).

panels.title: Funnel — upload → dispatch → job outcome (24 h)
Presigned URLs issued
panels.description: Successful GET /upload-url — presigned PUT URL returned, resume_imports row created (status=awaiting_upload).

panels.title: Lost uploads (S3 CORS / network gap)
panels.description: URLs issued minus jobs dispatched. > 0 means the browser PUT to S3 failed before /complete was called. Root cause: S3 CORS mis-match, presign expiry, or network error. The gap from the 2026-05-09 incident was caused by tucaken.io missing from the S3 CORS AllowedOrigins.

panels.title: Uploads dispatched
panels.description: POST /:id/complete returned 202 — S3 PUT succeeded and K8s Job was created.

panels.title: Jobs succeeded
panels.description: resume-import-processor Jobs that completed all pipeline steps and pushed outcome=success to Pushgateway.

panels.title: Jobs failed
panels.description: Jobs that exited with outcome=failed. Caused by PIPELINE_ERROR (caught in main), or by a crash before main() ran (MODULE_NOT_FOUND, CreateContainerConfigError) which leaves the import stuck at status=queued until manually reset.

panels.title: Job dispatch errors (502)
panels.description: POST /:id/complete returned 502 — K8s Job creation failed. Causes: image not resolved (ESO not yet synced), RBAC error, or namespace ResourceQuota exceeded.

panels.title: Infrastructure prerequisites — secrets, pod health, K8s job states
Secrets in resume-import ns
panels.description: Secrets present in the resume-import namespace (from kube-state-metrics). Expected: 2 required (platform-rds-credentials, admin-api-bedrock) + 1 optional (resume-import-secrets). Job pods use envFrom.secretRef — absent secrets cause CreateContainerConfigError. The 2026-05-09 incident: 0 secrets, fixed by applying ExternalSecret manifests.

panels.title: Job pods in error (waiting)
panels.description: Job pods in a waiting state due to CreateContainerConfigError (missing secret), ErrImagePull (ECR auth expired), or ImagePullBackOff. Any value > 0 means the job cannot start. Check the pod waiting reasons panel below for the specific reason.

panels.title: K8s jobs active now
panels.description: K8s Jobs currently running a resume-import-processor pod. Normally 0 between imports. > 0 during active processing. Stuck at > 0 for > 10 min may indicate a hung Bedrock call or pgbouncer connectivity issue.

panels.title: K8s jobs failed (not yet TTL-cleaned)
panels.description: Failed K8s Jobs still present (TTL = 3600s after completion). Each failed Job means backoffLimit was exhausted without a successful run. The 2026-05-09 incident produced 1 failed job: pod#1 CreateContainerConfigError, pod#2 MODULE_NOT_FOUND.

panels.title: K8s jobs succeeded (not yet TTL-cleaned)
panels.description: Succeeded K8s Jobs still present in the cluster (TTL = 3600s). Confirms the processor ran to completion.

panels.title: ECR token refresh last status
panels.description: ecr-token-refresh CronJob status. A failed run means ECR auth tokens may have expired, causing ErrImagePull or ImagePullBackOff on the next resume-import-processor Job pod. Observed failing 2026-05-09 during node shutdown.

panels.title: Job pod waiting reasons over time
panels.description: kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason for namespace=resume-import. CreateContainerConfigError = missing secret or configmap (fix: apply ExternalSecrets). ErrImagePull / ImagePullBackOff = ECR token expired (fix: force ecr-token-refresh). OOMKilled = increase container memory limit.

panels.title: K8s job status timeline
panels.description: Active / succeeded / failed job counts over time in the resume-import namespace. A spike in 'failed' with no 'succeeded' indicates the processor could not complete any run. Correlate with pod waiting reasons panel.

panels.title: Step-by-step — request rates through the upload flow
GET /upload-url — rate by status code
panels.description: 200 = URL issued + DB row created. 429 = free-tier quota hit. 415 = unsupported MIME. 413 = file too large. 503 = S3 bucket not configured (ASSETS_BUCKET_NAME unset — ESO not synced).

panels.title: POST /:id/complete — rate by status code
panels.description: 202 = K8s Job dispatched. 404 = import not found or already in progress. 502 = Job creation failed. A gap between /upload-url 200s and /complete 202s means the S3 PUT failed (CORS, expiry, network).

panels.title: Full pipeline chain — combined log view (admin-api → job pod)
Full chain — admin-api + job pod (time-ordered)
panels.description: Combined log stream from both admin-api (upload-url, complete, poll) and resume-import job pods. Shows the complete sequence: presign → dispatch → pod start → extraction → enrichment → completion. Use this panel to identify exactly where the pipeline stalled. Filter by trace_id to follow a single import end-to-end.

panels.title: K8s events — resume-import namespace
panels.description: Kubernetes Events for the resume-import namespace from eventrouter/Loki. Shows: FailedCreate (quota exceeded), CreateContainerConfigError (missing secret — fix: apply ExternalSecrets), BackOff (image pull failure), SuccessfulCreate (job pod started). Critical for diagnosing pre-start failures that never appear in the pod logs.

panels.title: ESO controller — ExternalSecret sync events
panels.description: External Secrets Operator controller logs filtered to resume-import namespace. Shows SecretSynced (healthy), SecretSyncedError (store unreachable or SSM path missing), and NotFound errors. The 2026-05-09 incident: ExternalSecret CRDs existed on disk but were never applied to the cluster — ESO had nothing to sync.

panels.title: Job processor — durations and pipeline outcomes
Job outcome rate (Pushgateway)
panels.description: resume_import_runs_total from Pushgateway. Only populated after a Job completes (success or caught-error). Jobs that crash before main() (MODULE_NOT_FOUND, CreateContainerConfigError) never reach the finally block and never push — use K8s job status panels above to detect those failures.

panels.title: Job end-to-end duration (p50 / p95)
panels.description: resume_import_duration_seconds from Pushgateway. Includes S3 download, parsing, Bedrock extraction, per-role enrichment, and embedding. p95 > 300 s may indicate Bedrock throttling or Tavily timeouts.

panels.title: Per-step duration (p95)
panels.description: resume_import_step_duration_seconds breakdown. Steps: s3_download, pdf_parse/docx_parse, bedrock_extract, enrichment_per_role, embed_persist. Identifies the bottleneck step.

panels.title: Enrichment entries processed
panels.description: resume_import_enrichment_entries_total. enriched = Bedrock + Tavily complete. skipped = no search results (null return from enrichRole). failed = enrichRole threw.

panels.title: GET /:id — status poll rate
panels.description: Frontend polls every 2-3 s from status=queued until completed or failed. A sustained high rate with no job completion indicates a stuck or crashed Job. If the job crashed before main(), the import stays at status=queued indefinitely — look for kube_job_status_failed > 0 and manually reset status=failed to unblock the retry endpoint.

panels.title: GET /upload-url latency (p50 / p95)
panels.description: Server-side latency for the upload URL endpoint. Includes quota DB check, plan lookup, S3 presign call, and resume_imports INSERT. p95 > 1 s may indicate pgbouncer or S3 presigner issues.

panels.title: admin-api — resume-import routes only
panels.description: Filtered to /api/admin/resume-imports/* routes. Shows upload-url requests (with presign timing), complete dispatch calls, retry calls, and poll responses. Use trace_id to correlate with the processor logs.

panels.title: resume-import-processor — K8s Job pod logs
panels.description: Logs from processor Job pods. Key messages: 'starting' (env vars loaded, pipeline begins), '[run-import] fetching from S3' (S3 download step), 'completed' (success, includes totalEmbeddings count), 'fatal error' (PIPELINE_ERROR, includes full err stack). Empty = pod never started (check K8s events panel).

panels.title: admin-api — /api/admin/resume-imports/* spans
panels.description: TraceQL for resume-import spans originating in admin-api. Covers: GET /upload-url (quota check + DB INSERT + S3 presign), POST /complete (status update + K8s Job create), GET /:id (poll). Click a trace to expand spans and see DB query timing and K8s API call duration.

targets.query: { resource.service.name="admin-api" && span.http.target =~ "/api/admin/resume-imports.*" } | select(span.http.target, span.http.status_code, span.http.method, span.url.path)

panels.title: resume-import-processor — pod-level pipeline spans
panels.description: TraceQL for spans from the resume-import-processor Job pod itself (OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=resume-import-processor, pushed via OTLP to alloy.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:4318). Each trace represents one Job run. Spans cover: s3_download, pdf_parse, bedrock_extract, enrichment_per_role, embed_persist. Empty when job crashes before OTel initialises (MODULE_NOT_FOUND, CreateContainerConfigError).

panels.title: Total duration
panels.description: resume_import_duration_seconds_sum for the selected importId (success or failed).

panels.title: Textract OCR sec
panels.description: Time spent in AWS Textract for this import (0 if pdf-parse succeeded).

panels.title: Bedrock extract sec
panels.description: Bedrock InvokeModel duration for extract_career on this import.

panels.title: Tavily total sec
panels.description: Sum of all Tavily searches for this import (one per role).

panels.title: Bedrock enrich sec
panels.description: Bedrock enrich_role duration (sum across all roles).

panels.title: Embed + persist sec
panels.description: Embedding generation + Postgres insert time.

panels.title: $importId — full pipeline timeline (Loki)
panels.description: All log lines from admin-api + processor for this importId / trace. Order ascending so the waterfall reads top → bottom.

targets.expr: {namespace=~"admin-api|resume-import"} | json | (import_id="$importId" or importId="$importId" or trace_id=~".*") | line_format "[{{.namespace}}] {{.event}} {{.msg}} {{.route}} {{.duration_ms}}ms {{.err}}"

panels.title: $importId — Tempo span waterfall
panels.description: Full distributed trace for resource.service.name=resume-import-processor with the chosen importId. Click into spans to see Textract / Bedrock / Postgres timing.

panels.title: Textract OCR duration p50/p95
panels.description: Histogram of Textract round-trip (StartDocumentTextDetection → completed).

panels.title: Tavily search duration p95 by outcome
panels.description: Tavily web-search latency split by outcome (success/empty/failed).

panels.title: Bedrock duration p95 by purpose
panels.description: Bedrock InvokeModel latency for extract vs enrich purposes.

panels.title: Embed + Postgres persist p95
panels.description: Embedding generation and DB insert latencies for the persist step.

panels.title: Bedrock spend by import (last 24 h)
panels.description: Joins resume_imports + bedrock_cost. Sums input/output token cost per import. Source: RDS.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
           ri.id            AS import_id,
           ri.status,
           ri.created_at    AS started,
           SUM(bc.input_tokens)  AS in_tokens,
           SUM(bc.output_tokens) AS out_tokens,
           ROUND(SUM(bc.input_tokens * bc.price_in_per_million + bc.output_tokens * bc.price_out_per_million) / 1e6, 4) AS usd
         FROM resume_imports ri
         LEFT JOIN bedrock_cost bc ON bc.import_id = ri.id
         WHERE ri.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
         GROUP BY ri.id, ri.status, ri.created_at
         ORDER BY ri.created_at DESC
         LIMIT 50

panels.title: Bedrock invocations by model (CloudWatch)
panels.description: AWS/Bedrock Invocations metric, summed per ModelId. Cross-reference with our histograms to spot model-side latency drift.

panels.title: Textract requests (CloudWatch)
panels.description: AWS/Textract SuccessfulRequestCount + ThrottledCount. Helps spot account-level Textract throttles separately from our retry behavior.

panels.title: Career entries extracted + embeddings created (24 h rate)
panels.description: Throughput of the pipeline — entries split by type, plus embedding row counter.

panels.title: Failure modes — split by reason / error_code / event
Failed jobs by error_code
panels.description: resume_import_runs_total{outcome=failed} broken down by error_code label.

panels.title: Textract fallback rate by reason
panels.description: How often pdf-parse fell through to Textract OCR, split by reason (threw / empty / short_text).

panels.title: enrich-role failures (Loki, 5 m rolling rate)
panels.description: Counts of structured events that mark a degraded enrichment outcome.

panels.title: admin-api resume-import error rate (4xx/5xx)
panels.description: Per-route error rate for the resume-import surface.

panels.title: Free-tier cap hits (24 h)
panels.description: Number of entries skipped due to FREE_TIER_ENRICHMENT_CAP. High value = upsell signal.

panels.title: Career entries extracted (24 h)
panels.description: Sum of experience+education+skill+cert+project+achievement entries created.

panels.title: Embeddings created (24 h)
panels.description: Embedding rows inserted into experience_embeddings.

panels.title: Textract fallback rate (24 h)
panels.description: Share of imports that needed OCR fallback. >50% = expect cost spike.
